spec_match.rationale: The lab is now honest that the nested extend behavior is illustrative and stored as data-focusgroup-intent because current Chrome requires behavior tokens and does not accept the older explainer-only extend spelling natively.

interactive.rationale: Four toggles update the intent strings live, the polyfill routes key presses according to those strings, and Chrome DevTools MCP verified horizontal and block-axis routing after toggles.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and note now frame the page as a focusgroup-style routing lab instead of claiming native behavior for unsupported token spellings.

edge_cases.rationale: The page explores wrap, block-vs-inline axis changes, nomemory, and illustrative extend, but still does not show a third nesting level or a side-by-side memory comparison.

references.rationale: Links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5637601087193088 and the Open UI explainer in see-also.

design_system.rationale: Variables drive the controls, notes, nested panels, and logs; there are no static-only cards.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate a third level of nesting
openQuestions.detail: Real menubars often nest three levels, such as menubar to menu to submenu. Add a third inner group so recursive nested routing is visible.

openQuestions.title: Show nomemory versus default memory side-by-side
openQuestions.detail: The nomemory token resets to the first item when the group is re-entered. Currently the visitor has to toggle the flag and re-Tab to see the difference; show two parallel groups so the reset behaviour is immediately visible.

summary: Re-tested in Chrome Canary through chrome-devtools-mcp at /v146/focusgroup/nested-focusgroup-lab/: the page loaded with no console/network errors, the nomemory and vertical-outer toggles changed data-focusgroup-intent, and ArrowRight/ArrowDown routing updated the focused item and log. The previous missing polyfill-disclosure question is resolved.
